name: Holiday Cookies (Bizcochos)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the lard in a large mixing bowl with a spoon or electric mixer until light and fluffy.
2. Gradually beat in 1/2 cup sugar.
3. Add the egg and orange zest.
4. Sift together the flour, baking powder, and salt into a separate bowl.
5. Add the sifted mixture to the creamed lard mixture about 1 cup at a time, beating well after each addition.
6. After adding 3 cups of flour, the dough should be smooth and workable with a slightly stiff consistency. If it is too soft to mix, add up to 1/2 cup more flour. If it is too hard to mix, add up to 2 tablespoons of orange juice.
7. Refrigerate the dough for 30 to 40 minutes until thoroughly chilled.
8. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough about 1/8-inch thick and cut with cookie cutters or pipe onto an ungreased baking sheet using a large pastry bag fitted with a No. 5 star tip.
9. Bake on an ungreased baking sheet at 350 degrees for about 10 to 12 minutes until edges are golden.
10. Combine the cinnamon and remaining 1/2 cup sugar in a bowl, then roll the cookies in the mixture.
Nutritional Information:
Each cookie contains approximately: 119 calories; 36 mg sodium; 18 mg cholesterol; 7 grams fat; 13 grams carbohydrates; 1 gram protein; 0.03 gram fiber.
